subject: 🏃 Thursday — Easy 3 mi (Soft Surface)
html:
Today: Easy run, 3 miles on a soft surface. Target pace 9:30–10:00/mi, heart rate under 140. Sawyer Camp Trail or Bayfront paths are your best options. This is your first run of the Chassis Build phase and your first run since the PT evaluation on April 17 — a week without impact. The goal is not fitness; it's a low-stakes check-in with the knee under load.

Your last run (April 16, 5 mi) flagged Yellow — medial knee awareness post-run. Tuesday's Upper Push session was Green, which is a good sign, but gym work doesn't load the knee the same way running does. Today tells us where things actually stand.

Before you head out: Do your daily mobility flush — T-spine extensions (PT), standing lunges with hip IR (PT), world's greatest stretch, 90/90, couch stretch, hip CARs, cat-cow. ~12 minutes. This is especially important before your first run back.

Focus cue: Knee awareness by mile. Note how the knee feels at mile 1, mile 2, and mile 3 separately. If awareness develops, note which mile it started and whether it's getting worse or staying flat. If it moves past "awareness" into discomfort at any point, cut the run short — no negotiating.

Context: Week 1 of 4 in Chassis Build — 3 weeks to your PT follow-up on May 15. You've done Hip & Core A (Monday), Upper Push (Tuesday), and Mobility Deep-Dive (Wednesday) so far this week. Tomorrow is Hip & Core B, then Upper Pull Saturday. The chassis sessions are the primary work; today's run is supplemental. Treat it that way.
